title: Plant Cell Structure
description: An interactive tour of a plant cell, highlighting the unique features that separate it from animal cells.

steps.speech: Welcome to the plant cell! While it shares many similarities with the animal cell we just saw, it has three very distinct features that allow plants to survive.
First is the rigid cell wall on the outside. This thick green barrier gives the plant structural support, allowing trees to grow hundreds of feet tall without a skeleton.
Notice that massive blue sac taking up most of the space? That's the central vacuole. It stores water and nutrients, and keeps the cell plump so the plant doesn't wilt.
These bright green ovals are chloroplasts. They are the solar panels of the cell, using sunlight to create food through photosynthesis.
Just like the animal cell, it also contains a nucleus to store DNA...
...and mitochondria to break down the food the chloroplasts make into usable energy.
Those three unique structures—the cell wall, vacuole, and chloroplasts—are what make plant life possible on Earth!
